title: 10 precautions to take when using Salvia officinalis (Sage) for medicinal purposes
intro: When using Salvia officinalis, or sage, for medicinal purposes, it is important to take several precautions. First, sage should not be used by women who are pregnant or breastfeeding, as it may stimulate uterine contractions or reduce milk flow. Additionally, sage should be used in moderation, as high doses or prolonged use can cause symptoms such as rapid heart rate, restlessness, tremors, and seizures. It is also recommended to consult with a healthcare provider before using sage for medicinal purposes, especially for those with existing health conditions or who are taking medications, as it can interact with certain drugs. Furthermore, it is important to use only high-quality, properly labeled sage from a reputable source, and to follow dosage instructions carefully. Finally, it is worth noting that while sage has a long history of use in traditional medicine, its medicinal properties and safety have not been fully studied, so it should be used with caution.
